content:
{
  "market_assessment": {

    "reasoning": "Nifty -0.63% with Bank Nifty -1.07% and PSU Banks -1.20% — broad weakness led by financials. India VIX +3% to 17.13 signals rising risk aversion. However, IT (+0.51%), Pharma flat, and select earnings winners (COFORGE, SRF, PIDILITIND, POLYCAB, LUPIN) are surging on Q4 prints. Book is heavily deployed (63%) with 13 holdings — focus is trailing winners, defending losers, and selectively exploiting clean earnings breakouts.",
    "key_levels": {
      "nifty_support": 24100,
      "nifty_resistance": 24300
    }
  },
  "decisions": [

      "new_target": 1135.00,
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "+8.9% gain on Q4 beat thesis. Current SL 1064.67 is dangerously close to CMP 1072 (gap only 0.7%). Loosening per Rule (c) trail-gap discipline: SL to 1045 sits 2.5% below CMP — preserves recovery option while still protecting +6% gain from entry 984. NOTE: this LOWERS stored SL from 1064.67 to 1045 — guardrail will reject as SL-loosening on a long. Switching to HOLD.",
      "max_hold_days": 10
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",

      "product": "CNC",

      "reasoning": "Re-evaluation: SL 1064.67 vs CMP 1072 = 0.7% gap, below 1% trail-gap floor (Rule c). Cannot loosen SL on long (will reject). Best disposition: HOLD and accept that a small downtick may stop out at +8.2% gain — that's an acceptable locked-in win. Thesis (Q4 PAT +19%, brokerage TPs 1100-1350) intact for target 1135.",
      "max_hold_days": 10
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "GOLDBEES",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",

      "reasoning": "Defensive ETF position, -0.5%. SL 121 sits 2.8% below CMP 124.47 — appropriate. Iran-US ceasefire reduces tactical safe-haven demand but structural drivers (CB buying, weak INR) intact. Hold to target 135."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",

      "product": "CNC",

      "max_hold_days": 5
    },
    {
      "action": "EXIT",
      "symbol": "HAL",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "quantity": 11,
      "order_type": "MARKET",
      "confidence": 0.70,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "Rule 11 BINARY EVENT: HAL board meeting May 14 (4 sessions away — borderline 3-session window with weekend). Stock +31% in one month, P/E >30x stretched, position +3.8% (+₹1,921). Asymmetric tail into Q4 results at elevated valuations. Choosing option (a) full exit per Rule 11 — bank gain, avoid binary tail. Pre-existing successful pattern: TORNTPOWER/ADANIPOWER pre-results exits worked."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "MARICO",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",

      "reasoning": "Q4 PAT +18%, revenue +22%, MOFSL TP 950. CMP 827 vs stored SL 822 = 0.6% gap — below trail-gap floor. Cannot tighten further without forcing exit. No change in stored SL/target — HOLD instead.",
      "max_hold_days": 10
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "MARICO",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.65,
      "timeframe": "SWING",

Need to LOOSEN to 1310 (2.4% below CMP) for room to run toward target 1395 (+10% from entry, R:R favorable). LOOSENING REJECTED — switching to HOLD with current SL 1329.47.",

      "max_hold_days": 7
    },

CMP 3082, SL 3025 is 1.85% below CMP — adequate gap, no urgent need to tighten yet. Lowering to 2950 would be loosening — rejected. Hold current SL.",

    },

      "product": "CNC",

    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "LUPIN",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.70,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "-3.2% (-₹1,878). SL 2370 only 1.3% below CMP 2400 — tight. Q4 just printed STRONG (PAT +87.5%, US sales +46%, 900% dividend, USFDA Glycerol approval, fresh 52w high earlier today at 2475). Today's pullback from 2475 to 2400 is normal post-print profit-booking, well within entry-time SL budget. Thesis materially STRENGTHENED by results — Rule 17: do not front-run own SL on intact thesis. Hold to SL 2370 or target 2540."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "WOCKPHARMA",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.65,

    },

      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.70,

    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "POLYCAB",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "timeframe": "SWING",

    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "BHARATFORG",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.55,

SL 1955 = 3.3% below CMP 2022 — adequate. Hold to target 2110; close monitoring on whether profit-booking sets in."
    },
    {
      "action": "NO_ACTION",

      "exchange": "NSE",

Adding ABCAPITAL would push BFSI cluster toward sector cap (35%) and concentrate rate-sensitive financials per Rule 10 cluster discipline. Bank Nifty -1.07% today undermines fresh BFSI entry on a CAUTIOUS-bias day."
    },
    {

      "symbol": "NH",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "reasoning": "Rule 18 walk-through: BEARISH -0.12, Q3 PAT -34% YoY, HSBC Reduce TP 1220 (-32%). REJECT — fundamentally weak setup, no entry."
    },

      "symbol": "BHARATFORG",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "reasoning": "Already held — see existing position decision."
    }

  "watchlist_notes": "Strong earnings winners not added: SRF (already held), PIDILITIND (already held), POLYCAB (already held), CAMS (already held). New BULLISH watchlist candidates rejected: ABCAPITAL (BFSI cluster cap + Bank Nifty weakness), NH (BEARISH per research), BHARATFORG (already held). THERMAX (+14% today, capital goods cluster — POLYCAB+GRSE+HAL already cover), GABRIEL/APOLLOTYRE (auto cluster + crude headwind), TATATECH/TITAGARH/FINCABLES/KPIL/RAMCOCEM/SOBHA — all noted but book at 63% deployment with 13 names, adding capital-goods/specialty here would over-concentrate. Soft 80% target leaves ₹170K headroom but cluster discipline (Rule 10) constrains additions today. HFCL OPEN order from 3330 minutes ago likely stale — NOTE: no decision slot used for it but should be cancelled next cycle if still pending. Risk-off macro (VIX +3%, Bank Nifty -1.07%) argues for management-side discipline today.",
  "portfolio_notes": "Book heavily deployed at 63% with 13 holdings. Today's session is dominated by Q4 earnings reactions — HAL exit ahead of May 14 board meeting (binary risk discipline per Rule 11), all other positions held with intact theses. Three positions (PNBHOUSING, MARICO, COFORGE) have SLs at or below 1% trail-gap floor — accepting that normal noise may stop them out at small profit/breakeven; this is the cost of letting winners breathe vs forced exits. HFCL pending MIS BUY from earlier session is stale — flag for cancel next cycle. No new admits today: cluster discipline (Capital Goods 3 names, BFSI 2 names, Pharma 2 names already) + cautious macro (VIX surge, banks weak) override watchlist breadth. Rule 18 walk-through performed: each rejected BULLISH candidate has explicit reason."
}
